jokes.text: What the fuck are you doing?! You gotta get outta my car. I'm serious!
jokes.punchline: Abrupt angry reaction to dancing passenger
jokes.explanation: Sharp contrast between expected commercial joy and realistic driver annoyance. Good economy of language.

jokes.text: That crazy dancin' makin' my penis soft.
jokes.punchline: Unexpected anatomical complaint
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and unexpected complaint. Very quotable line with perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: Now, that is what I call dancin'! You should've seen the girl that was sittin' in there before you
jokes.setup: Previous passenger made his penis soft
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic praise for new dancer after harsh criticism
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to previous scene but not as memorable as the setup.

jokes.text: It's a bleeper of a blooper, though.
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on bleeper/blooper for censorship
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay, functional but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Look at my face when I see her titty pop out.
jokes.punchline: Dave's exaggerated facial reaction to wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy enhanced by Dave's self-aware commentary. Good physical reaction.

jokes.text: I felt guilty, like I did that with my mind. 'Cause the whole time she was dancin', I was like, "come on, titty, come on, titty!"
jokes.punchline: Admitting to mentally willing it to happen
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about male psychology. The repetition of 'come on, titty' is hilarious and quotable.

jokes.text: Man, I got here when I could! Shit, you're not my fucking moms!
jokes.setup: Training video about being late to work
jokes.punchline: Aggressive, inappropriate response to supervisor
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of terrible customer service training. Character-specific dialogue that feels authentic to the satire.

jokes.text: I don't give a fuck, I'll go to Rikers for three or four years just to prove my point
jokes.setup: Employee telling threatening story to customer
jokes.punchline: Extreme prison threat over minor issue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of inappropriate workplace behavior. Specific prison reference adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Hell, yeah, I suck toes!
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic admission of foot fetish
jokes.explanation: Shocking and uncomfortable in exactly the right way for the sketch premise. Very quotable.

jokes.text: My butt is itchin' like crazy, and I took a shower.
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about personal hygiene issues
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of inappropriate oversharing but less memorable than previous examples.

jokes.text: If they got that, tell them the computers are down. They should be, anyhow.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that computers don't work anyway
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of systematic incompetence. The casual 'should be anyhow' sells it.

jokes.text: Listen, sister, I gotta go take a shit.
jokes.setup: Customer asking about system being down
jokes.punchline: Crude dismissal to use bathroom
jokes.explanation: Effective shock humor but relies heavily on crude language rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Guess what, nigga? I am your manager, what's up?
jokes.punchline: Aggressive confrontational response as manager
jokes.explanation: Escalates the bad service concept effectively. The aggressive 'what's up?' is well-delivered.

jokes.text: 'Cause fuck 'em, that's why.
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest, simple answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline with maximum economy. The simple, direct answer is both shocking and hilarious.

jokes.text: spray chocolate sauce on the wall near the toilet to give the appearance of errant feces
jokes.punchline: Deliberately making bathroom look dirty with fake poop
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept. The formal phrasing 'errant feces' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You graduated from grade school, and you don't have to take shit from anyone.
jokes.punchline: Grade school as ultimate achievement justifying bad behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of false empowerment. Grade school as peak achievement is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: It's not HBO... It's just regular-ass TV.
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of lower production values
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware comedy. Perfect economy and timing, very quotable.

jokes.text: 'Cause it's not like women were objectified when hip-hop came out. We've been objectifying women for centuries. I'm tired of hip-hop takin' the rap.
jokes.setup: Defending hip-hop against claims of objectifying women
jokes.punchline: 'Taking the rap' pun while making historical point about objectification
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural commentary with wordplay, sets up the Nat King Cole bit

jokes.text: Happy Holidays, you triflin' bitch!
jokes.setup: Elegant Christmas performance by Nat King Cole
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to crude hip-hop language
jokes.explanation: Shocking and brilliant juxtaposition. The contrast between elegant performance and crude language is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Rub it in your breasts and on your vagina. Yes, yes, I am horny, that's sexy TV...
jokes.setup: Nat King Cole doing Christmas special
jokes.punchline: Explicit sexual commentary in 1950s style
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the concept but not as sharp as the initial shock. Still effective character work.

jokes.text: We sip only the best Dom P., because I'm smooth like that. Peace, you punk bitches.
jokes.setup: Nat King Cole acting like a modern rapper
jokes.punchline: Bragging about expensive champagne and calling audience bitches
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the anachronistic rapper persona

jokes.text: Janice: 'what are you saying, Brian?' Brian: 'I am afraid, I just feel like someday you might look like your mom, maybe.'
jokes.setup: Couple arguing about what was said
jokes.punchline: Stenographer reading back the hurtful comment about looking like her mom
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff for the product concept, relationship humor

jokes.text: I'm gonna go masturbate, Home Stenographer. Wanna write that down?
jokes.punchline: Deliberately saying something embarrassing to be recorded
jokes.explanation: Good use of the premise but relies on shock value. The 'wanna write that down?' addition is nice.

jokes.text: All right you little brats, go to sleep, my boyfriend's here, we're gonna have us a sex session.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing about sexual plans
jokes.explanation: Effective premise use but somewhat predictable. The casual 'sex session' phrasing works well.

jokes.text: I guess, if it came down to it, I would bang Ben Affleck.
jokes.punchline: Compromising statement about celebrity
jokes.explanation: Decent premise execution but relies on celebrity name-drop. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Stupid dwarf! Totally gay, 100%.
jokes.setup: Friend realizing his gay admission was recorded
jokes.punchline: Calling the stenographer a dwarf and declaring his friend totally gay
jokes.explanation: Crude reaction humor, relies on slurs and juvenile gay panic

jokes.text: I showed it to a black friend of mine. He looked at me like I had set black people back with a comedy sketch.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of potentially problematic content
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware comedy that acknowledges the controversy. Shows Chappelle's understanding of his material's impact.

jokes.text: What, you don't think I can write them books? Just 'cause I'm blind don't mean I'm dumb.
jokes.setup: Reporter questioning if he's the author
jokes.punchline: Defensive response missing the real issue (his race)
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work. The irony of him missing the obvious while being defensive about blindness is brilliant.

jokes.text: Well, he was the only nigra we'd ever had around here, so we figured we'd make it easier on Clayton by just telling him and all the other blind kids that he was white.
jokes.setup: Explanation of how this situation happened
jokes.punchline: Casually explaining the incredibly inappropriate solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. The casual delivery of such an outrageous backstory is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: They're lazy, good-for-nothing tricksters, crack-smoking swindlers, big-butt havin', wide nose breathin' all the white man's air.
jokes.setup: Asked about his problem with African Americans
jokes.punchline: Racist rant delivered by a black man
jokes.explanation: The core irony of the sketch executed perfectly. 'Breathin' all the white man's air' is particularly absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: Anyone have sex with my sister, it's gonna be me!
jokes.setup: Story about friend protecting sister from black date
jokes.punchline: Incest threat delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that perfectly captures the character's twisted logic. Extremely quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: He said, 'look here, nigger, that there's my girl. Anyone have sex with my sister, it's gonna be me!'
jokes.setup: Clayton telling story about his friend Jasper
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of incest as the punchline to racist anecdote
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that adds incest to the racist story, very dark humor

jokes.text: I am in no way, shape, or form involved in any niggerdom, you understand?!
jokes.punchline: Vehement denial using made-up word 'niggerdom'
jokes.explanation: Great made-up word that sounds both official and ridiculous. Character's vehemence makes it funnier.

jokes.text: If I tell him he's black, he'd probably kill hisself just so there'd be one less nigger around.
jokes.setup: Why doesn't friend tell Clayton he's black
jokes.punchline: Clayton's racism would extend to self-destruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that reveals the logical extreme of the character's commitment to racism.

jokes.text: White power! Nigger...
jokes.setup: Clayton sees his reflection in car window
jokes.punchline: Starts to call himself the slur but stops, confused
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of cognitive dissonance. The economy of the reaction and the visual gag work brilliantly together.

jokes.text: Hey, why don't you jungle bunnies turn that music down?! Nigras make me sick! Woogie-boogie, nigger! Woogie-boogie!
jokes.punchline: Clayton yelling racist comments at them
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency but the 'woogie-boogie' repetition feels slightly forced.

jokes.text: Did he just call us 'niggers'? Awesome!
jokes.punchline: Positive reaction because it came from black person
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twist on expectations. The enthusiastic 'Awesome!' is perfectly timed and unexpected.

jokes.text: America's at war with Al Qaeda, but we're still losing the war against Al Sharpton!
jokes.setup: America at war with Al Qaeda
jokes.punchline: Claiming bigger threat is Al Sharpton
jokes.explanation: Good parallel structure and perfectly in character. The Al/Al wordplay works well.

jokes.text: Let's talk about Chinese people, with their Kung Fu and all that silly 'ching chang chong' talk. I can't understand you, go back to your country.
jokes.punchline: Crude stereotyping from black white supremacist
jokes.explanation: Consistent character work but relies on basic stereotypes. Less clever than other moments.

jokes.text: If you don't like Will & Grace, that don't mean there's something wrong with you. It means there's something wrong with Will! He's a homosexual.
jokes.setup: Don't let media tell you how to think
jokes.punchline: Using Will & Grace to make homophobic point
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific cultural reference but not as sharp as other political commentary.

jokes.text: 'Cunnilingus' Rice sounds like a Mexican dish, maybe we should put her on a plate and send her to Mexico so the Mexicans will eat her... white power.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Mexican food fantasy
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation but feels forced and rambling compared to tighter material.

jokes.text: Because she's a nigger lover.
jokes.punchline: Ultimate racist logic applied to himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline that completes the sketch's logic brilliantly. The ultimate expression of the character's impossible position - pure comedic genius.

jokes.text: White power! That's right, I hate niggers, but I love skateboards.
jokes.punchline: Adding random skateboard preference
jokes.explanation: Random character detail that's mildly amusing but doesn't add much after the perfect divorce punchline.

jokes.text: That's very hard, to ignore the hundreds of people in front of me and just pay attention to the theoretical motherfuckers that may or may not watch the show for all I know.
jokes.setup: Supposed to talk to TV audience not live audience
jokes.punchline: Calling TV viewers 'theoretical motherfuckers'
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on television performance. 'Theoretical motherfuckers' is a great phrase.

jokes.text: 'White power my... pssht. That's white power, I have the power to change the station.'
jokes.punchline: White power redefined as remote control power
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay callback to the sketch. Nice way to diffuse tension while being self-aware about controversy.
